info.title: Altair API
info.description: Personal OS API for managing knowledge, goals, and resources

get.description: Placeholder endpoint - not yet implemented. This will list all attachments in the future.

501.description: Not Implemented - This endpoint is a placeholder for future implementation

get.description:
This endpoint returns the user profile for the authenticated user.
Authentication is enforced by the `AuthenticatedUser` extractor.

# Returns

Returns a JSON response with the user data:
- `id`: User's UUID
- `name`: Display name
- `email`: Email address
- `email_verified`: Whether email is verified
- `image`: Profile image URL (optional)
- `created_at`: Account creation timestamp
- `updated_at`: Last update timestamp

# Errors

Returns 401 Unauthorized if:
- Session token cookie is missing
- Session token is invalid
- Session has expired

These errors are handled automatically by the `AuthenticatedUser` extractor.

get.summary: List all households accessible to the authenticated user.
get.description:
Returns households where the user is either:
- The owner (`owner_user_id = user_id`)
- An active member (via `household_memberships`)

Soft-deleted households (`deleted_at IS NOT NULL`) are excluded.

post.summary: Create a new household with automatic owner membership.
post.description:
Creates both:
1. The household record
2. A household_memberships record with role='owner', is_active=true

Uses a database transaction to ensure atomicity.

get.summary: Get a single household by ID.
get.description:
Returns 404 if:
- Household doesn't exist
- Household is soft-deleted
- User is neither owner nor active member

404.description: Household not found or access denied

delete.description:
Only the household owner can delete the household.
Sets `deleted_at = NOW()` instead of removing the record.

403.description: User is not the household owner

patch.description:
Only the household owner can update the household.
Soft-deleted households cannot be updated.

403.description: User is not the household owner

get.summary: List all members of a household.
get.description:
Returns 404 if:
- Household doesn't exist
- Household is soft-deleted
- User is neither owner nor active member

403.description: User is not a member of the household

get.summary: List all initiatives accessible to the authenticated user.
get.description:
Returns initiatives where the user is either:
- The owner (`owner_user_id = user_id`)
- An active member of the initiative's household (via `household_memberships`)

Soft-deleted initiatives (`deleted_at IS NOT NULL`) are excluded.

200.description: List of initiatives accessible to the authenticated user

post.description:
Creates a single initiative record with the authenticated user as owner.
If a household_id is provided, validates that the user is an active member.

400.description: Bad request - invalid household or data

get.summary: Get a single initiative by ID.
get.description:
Returns 404 if:
- Initiative doesn't exist
- Initiative is soft-deleted
- User is neither owner nor active household member

403.description: Forbidden - user does not have access to this initiative

404.description: Initiative not found or access denied

delete.description:
Only the initiative owner can delete the initiative.
Sets `deleted_at = NOW()` instead of removing the record.

403.description: Forbidden - only initiative owner can delete

patch.description:
Only the initiative owner can update the initiative.
Soft-deleted initiatives cannot be updated.

403.description: Forbidden - only initiative owner can update

get.summary: List all relations accessible to the authenticated user.
get.description:
Returns relations where user is either:
- The owner (`owner_user_id = user_id`)
- An active member of relation's household (via `household_memberships`)

Supports filtering by `from_entity_id` and `to_entity_id` query parameters.
Soft-deleted relations (`deleted_at IS NOT NULL`) are excluded.

post.description:
Creates a single relation record with the authenticated user as owner.
Validates that both `from_entity_id` and `to_entity_id` exist
as valid entity IDs in their respective tables.

If a household_id is provided, validates that user is an active member.

400.description: Invalid request - entities don't exist or duplicate relation

get.summary: Get a single relation by ID.
get.description:
Returns 404 if:
- Relation doesn't exist
- Relation is soft-deleted
- User is neither owner nor active household member

delete.description:
Only relation owner can delete the relation.
Sets `deleted_at = NOW()` instead of removing the record.

patch.description:
Only relation owner can update the status.
Allowed status values are: active, suspended, deleted

get.summary: List all tags accessible to the authenticated user.
get.description: List all tags accessible to the authenticated user. Returns tags where the user is the owner or an active member of the tag's household.

post.description: Create a new tag with the authenticated user as owner. If a household_id is provided, validates that the user is an active member. If slug is not provided, generates one from the name.

400.description: Bad request - invalid input or not a household member

get.summary: Get a single tag by ID.
get.description: Get a single tag by ID. User must be the owner or an active member of the tag's household. Returns 404 for soft-deleted tags.

403.description: Forbidden - user cannot access this tag

delete.description: Soft delete a tag. Only the tag owner can delete the tag. Sets deleted_at = NOW() instead of removing the record.

403.description: Forbidden - only tag owner can delete

404.description: Tag not found or already soft-deleted

patch.description: Update a tag. Only the tag owner can update the tag. Soft-deleted tags cannot be updated.

403.description: Forbidden - only tag owner can update

get.summary: Health check endpoint that verifies database connectivity.
get.description:
Executes a simple `SELECT 1` query against the database to ensure
the connection pool is functioning correctly.

# Returns

Returns a JSON response with:
- `status`: "healthy"
- `database`: "connected"
- `timestamp`: ISO 8601 formatted timestamp

get.summary: Get the currently authenticated user's profile.
get.description:
This endpoint returns the application-specific user profile for the authenticated user.
Authentication is enforced by the `AuthenticatedUser` extractor.

# Returns

Returns a JSON response with the user data:
- `id`: User's UUID
- `email`: Email address
- `display_name`: Display name
- `timezone`: User's timezone preference
- `is_active`: Whether the user account is active
- `created_at`: Account creation timestamp
- `updated_at`: Last update timestamp

# Errors

Returns 401 Unauthorized if:
- Session token cookie is missing
- Session token is invalid
- Session has expired

Returns 404 Not Found if:
- User profile does not exist in the custom users table

These errors are handled automatically by the `AuthenticatedUser` extractor
and database query.

AppUser.description:
User model representing the custom `users` table.

Contains application-specific user data:
- id: UUID primary key (matches Better-Auth user.id)
- email: Email address
- display_name: User's display name
- timezone: User's timezone preference
- is_active: Whether the user account is active
- created_at: Account creation timestamp
- updated_at: Last update timestamp

CreateHouseholdRequest.description: Request body for creating a new household.

CreateInitiativeRequest.description: Request body for creating a new initiative.

CreateRelationRequest.description: Request body for creating a new relation.

confidence.description: Optional confidence score for inferred relations (0.0 to 1.0)

evidence.description: Optional evidence JSON for inferred relations

from_entity_type.description: Type of source entity (user, household, initiative, tag, attachment, epic, quest, routine, focus_session, note, location, category, item)

household_id.description: Optional household ID for shared relations

relation_type.description: Type of relationship (parent_of, child_of, relates_to, depends_on, blocks, blocked_by, duplicates, similar_to, references, contains, owned_by, assigned_to, part_of, precedes, succeeds)

source_type.description: How this relation was established (manual, inferred, imported, system)

to_entity_type.description: Type of target entity (user, household, initiative, tag, attachment, epic, quest, routine, focus_session, note, location, category, item)

CreateTagRequest.description: Request body for creating a new tag.

ErrorResponse.description: Error response format for authentication failures.

HouseholdMember.description: Household member with user details for membership listing.

HouseholdMembership.description: Household membership model representing the `household_memberships` table.

UpdateHouseholdRequest.description: Request body for updating a household.

UpdateInitiativeRequest.description: Request body for updating an initiative.

UpdateStatusRequest.description: Request body for updating relation status.

status.description: New status for the relation (active, suspended, deleted)

UpdateTagRequest.description: Request body for updating a tag.

User.description:
User model representing the `user` table from Better-Auth.

Matches the Better-Auth `user` schema:
- id: UUID primary key
- name: Display name (required)
- email: Email address (required, unique)
- email_verified: Whether email has been verified (default false)
- image: Profile image URL (optional)
- created_at: Account creation timestamp
- updated_at: Last update timestamp
